sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the current board state, the layout has changed significantly. The board now has:
- Green blocks scattered in various positions
- Yellow dots at rows 17 (cols 38, 40, 44, 46, 50, 52) and rows 59 (cols 14, 16, 20, 22, 26, 28)
- Character at rows 37-40, cols 44-45
- Blue region covering most of the board (rows 13-62, cols 13-53)
- Some additional blue region at rows 55-62, cols 13-29
The board seems to have transformed after removing enough green blocks. Now I need to:
1. Continue removing green blocks
2. Figure out the new pattern
Looking at the green blocks:
- 2 at top: rows 1-5, 7-11, cols 31-35
- 3 at middle: rows 31-35, cols 13-17, 37-41, 43-47
- 4 at rows 37-41, cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 31-35
- 4 at rows 43-47, cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 31-35
Total: 13 green blocks
The character is at cols 44-45, which is on the right side. I need to continue clicking on green blocks to remove them.
But wait, looking at the image, I see pink/purple blocks with yellow dots at the middle of the board. These might be the targets I need to navigate to after removing all green blocks.
Let me continue clicking on the remaining green blocks. The character is at cols 44-45, which is on the right side. I should be able to click on green blocks from this position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Board transformed. Now have 13 green blocks scattered.
# Character at cols 44-45 (right side)
# Need to continue removing green blocks

# Green blocks:
# rows 1-5, cols 31-35
# rows 7-11, cols 31-35
# rows 31-35, cols 13-17, 37-41, 43-47
# rows 37-41, cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 31-35
# rows 43-47, cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 31-35

# Click on all remaining green blocks
clicks = [
    (3, 33), (9, 33),  # top 2
    (33, 15), (33, 39), (33, 45),  # middle row
    (39, 15), (39, 21), (39, 27), (39, 33),  # rows 37-41
    (45, 15), (45, 21), (45, 27), (45, 33)  # rows 43-47
]

for r, c in clicks:
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c}])
    green_count = sum(1 for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N')
    print(f"Click ({r},{c}): green={green_count}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
    if last_action_result.get('game_over') or last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        break

print(f"\nFinal: level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['B', 'G', 'p', 'W']:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
